changes.detail: All five mandatory fields present: work_id=D-7, branch=feature/wave1-liver-cleanup, risk_level=HIGH, execution_model=TWO_PHASE_START_FINISH, change_type=BEHAVIOUR. Classification correct — BEHAVIOUR covers changes to `liv_consequence()` fallback (changes emitted `consequence_sentence` text) and `Wave1DomainCards.tsx` label rendering (changes what user sees in governed rendering surface). HIGH risk is correct: domain_narrative_wave1.py is in backend/core/analytics/, which is an Intelligence Core path.

Liver consequence defect confirmed at exact source location. `liv_consequence_primary(by_id, primary_idl)` at line 477: when primary_idl is None (no active liver IDL record), falls to `return liv_consequence(by_id)` at line 482. `liv_consequence(by_id)` at line 564: checks `ph_hepatic_alt_inflammatory_v1` severity_state; when severity_state IS 'not_observed' (enzymes within range), the active-IDL branch at line 567-568 does NOT fire. Falls to line 569-570: `t = governed_idl_field(_ID_HEP, 'why_it_matters'); return t or ''`. This reads the STATIC YAML `why_it_matters` regardless of runtime severity_state — returning 'Early liver strain patterns are a key lane toward MASLD/fibrosis risk if ignored.' even when ALT/hepatic pattern is not_observed. This is the exact defect: static KB text emitted as if active. `liv_consequence()` has exactly one caller in the backend: `liv_consequence_primary()` at line 482. Fix is bounded.

Label rendering defect confirmed. At lines 125-136: `d.missing_marker_ids.map((m) => (
  • {m}
    • ))`. Raw internal ID `m` is rendered verbatim with font-mono class — no label transformation. IDs seen live in UAT: 'ast', 'ggt', 'total_bilirubin'. These are biomarker SSOT canonical keys emitted by `liv_block()` at domain_score_assembler.py. The assembler adds: `for m in ('ast', 'ggt', 'alp', 'albumin', 'total_bilirubin'): if m not in panel_biomarker_ids and m not in missing: missing.append(m)`. These raw IDs flow through ConsumerDomainScoreV1.missing_marker_ids and are rendered without transformation. Fix point is confirmed as Wave1DomainCards.tsx:131-134.

Canonical label source confirmed present in SSOT. biomarkers.yaml has `description` fields for liver markers: ast.description='Aspartate aminotransferase' (line 402), ggt.description='Gamma-glutamyl transferase' (line 1424), bilirubin.description='Total bilirubin' (line 1467), alp.description='Alkaline phosphatase' (line 1444). These provide governed human-readable text. However: the SSOT canonical key is 'bilirubin' (not 'total_bilirubin') — but the assembler emits 'total_bilirubin' as a missing_marker_id. This creates a SSOT key mismatch. The label mapping must handle 'total_bilirubin' explicitly as an alias for the governed 'Total bilirubin' label, or the fix must normalise to the SSOT key. Prompt requirement 'if there is already a canonical/alias source in the repo that should drive this, use it' applies to these description fields.

The hardcoded missing-marker supplement in liv_block() confirmed as the source of 'total_bilirubin' in missing_marker_ids. The list ('ast', 'ggt', 'alp', 'albumin', 'total_bilirubin') is appended at assembly time. 'total_bilirubin' does not match any SSOT top-level key (SSOT key is 'bilirubin'). D-7 Cursor must be aware: if it attempts to look up 'total_bilirubin' in biomarkers.yaml by top-level key, the lookup will fail. Label mapping must treat 'total_bilirubin' as a known case — either via explicit map entry or by normalising the assembler to emit 'bilirubin' instead. The latter (normalising the assembler) is out of scope per D-7 constraints unless Cursor judges it minimal and safe.

Root cause is `governed_idl_field(_ID_HEP, 'why_it_matters')` at line 569 as the last fallback in `liv_consequence()`. This function reads directly from the static IDL YAML (bypassing the runtime IDL bundle and its published severity_state). When the runtime IDL has severity_state='not_observed' (normal enzymes), the dynamic check at line 567 does not fire, and the static YAML `why_it_matters` is returned — which contains active-strain language regardless. The fix must replace this final fallback with a neutral proportionate string for the no-active-pattern case. The stronger IDL-backed consequence must still appear when the live IDL record is active (severity_state in 'watch', 'attention', 'strong_signal'). This is a one- or two-line targeted fix in `liv_consequence()` — does not require reopening the D-6 primary-pattern architecture.

Four test targets explicitly named in prompt: (1) neutral/proportionate liver consequence when no active liver-risk pattern resolved; (2) stronger liver consequence when resolved pattern genuinely supports it; (3) missing/improver labels rendered as user-safe labels not raw ids; (4) type-check for touched surfaces. These are the minimal behavioral regression targets. Tests 1 and 2 require an IDL bundle fixture with `ph_hepatic_alt_inflammatory_v1` at severity_state='not_observed' vs 'attention'/'watch'. The InterpretationDisplayLayerBundleV1 fixture pattern from existing test files applies. Test 3 requires a rendering test for Wave1DomainCards with known missing_marker_ids.

Forbidden scope confirmed: cardiovascular logic, blood sugar logic, backfill architecture, D-6 primary-pattern selector, 'What's driving this' architecture, Phase 2 domains, broader results-page redesign. Forbidden files: orchestrator.py, backfill runner, pricing/billing, upload flow, clinician PDF/export, Phase 2 domain logic, broad KB/SSOT changes. STOP conditions at lines 247-254 protect against reopening the Wave 1 architecture or discovering a hemoglobin/unit issue. No STOP condition triggered by source read.

Liver consequence fix replaces a static YAML field read with either another static governed string (neutral fallback) or the runtime IDL-backed consequence. Both paths are deterministic — no LLM, no randomness. The condition (severity_state check) is already deterministic and identical to the pattern used in cv_consequence_primary() and met_consequence_primary(). No drift risk introduced. Label mapping in the frontend is a static lookup table — fully deterministic.

advisory_notes: TOTAL_BILIRUBIN vs SSOT BILIRUBIN: The assembler emits 'total_bilirubin' as a missing_marker_id (hardcoded in liv_block()) but the SSOT canonical key is 'bilirubin' (description: 'Total bilirubin'). Any label map using SSOT description-field lookup by canonical key will not find 'total_bilirubin'. The map must include an explicit 'total_bilirubin' -> 'Total bilirubin' entry, or the assembler must be corrected to emit 'bilirubin'. If Cursor chooses to fix the assembler key, it must verify no downstream code depends on 'total_bilirubin' as a literal string.

LIV_CONSEQUENCE FIX SCOPE: The fix to `liv_consequence()` at line 569-570 only affects the final fallback — when ph_hepatic_alt_inflammatory_v1 is not_observed AND primary_idl is None. The IDL-backed consequence path (lines 566-568) is unchanged. Cursor must confirm the neutral fallback string is proportionate and not falsely reassuring — per prompt constraint: 'Do not make liver falsely reassuring. The goal is coherence, not optimism.'
SINGLE CALLER: `liv_consequence()` has exactly one caller — `liv_consequence_primary()` at line 482. No other backend call site. Fix is fully bounded.

SSOT DESCRIPTION FIELDS AS LABEL SOURCE: biomarkers.yaml description fields (ast='Aspartate aminotransferase', ggt='Gamma-glutamyl transferase', bilirubin='Total bilirubin', alp='Alkaline phosphatase', albumin — check for entry) provide governed text. Frontend label map may reference these values without reading YAML at runtime — a static TypeScript map derived from SSOT values is the cleanest bounded implementation.
